chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram

text: 2596 - وَ سَأَلَهُ حَمَّادٌ اَلنَّوَّاءُ أَوْ سُئِلَ وَ هُوَ حَاضِرٌ : عَنِ اَلْمُحْرِمِ يُحْرِمُ فِي بُرْدٍ قَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ وَ هَلْ كَانَ اَلنَّاسُ يُحْرِمُونَ إِلاَّ فِي اَلْبُرُودِ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2596 - Hammad al-Nawwa’ asked, or was present when someone asked, about a Muhrim entering Ihram while wearing a striped cloak (burd).
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it. Were people not entering Ihram except in striped cloaks?"
